item.title: word_count
item.description: Vault Stats This vault currently contains 15,048 words across all notes.

item.title: Lapel
item.description: General Usually in clothing the lapel is the “chest” area, but in BJJ it is referring to the end of the clothing below the Belt.

item.title: Knee on belly
item.description: For defence see: Knee on belly defence Grips on opponent lapel (upper torso) and waist (pants). One leg on solar plexus, ankle at opponents thigh.

item.title: Rear naked choke
item.description: RNC is initiated from a back position with a Seatbelt grip Get hooks in: Goal is to solidify your back position using legs as hooks.

item.title: Berimbolo
item.description: General Berimbolo is essentially an attack system to take the back.

item.title: Sweep
item.description: General Sweep is anything that sweeps the opponent your top position to a worse one.

item.title: Sumi Gaeshi
item.description: General A type of Sacrifice throw originating from Judo.

item.title: Shoot
item.description: General Shooting is generally the motion of rapidly getting close to opponents waist in Standing, which often leads to Single leg or Double leg Steps Start from standing and ensure there are no grips currently blocking or even slowing down movement.

item.title: Base
item.description: General Imagine that you have a direct line outward from the center of your torso. That is zone where you can influence your opponent the most.
